purpose: Improve template variable handling so it becomes clearer, more scalable, better documented and safer across templates, blueprints, teams and future work profiles.
goal: Template variables should be centrally defined, grouped by context, well documented, previewable, and easier to extend without breaking existing templates.
non_goals: Do not break existing templates abruptly.
Do not duplicate separate variable definitions in frontend and backend.
Do not couple context validation with hidden runtime-only assumptions.
architecture_constraints: Keep one canonical variable registry that can be consumed by backend, frontend and tests.
Preserve hub-owned orchestration and avoid worker-to-worker control paths.
Introduce changes additively with aliases and migration notes before hard removals.
success_criteria: Template variables are no longer just a flat hardcoded list without structure.
Users can understand which variables exist, what they mean and in which context they are available.
The system supports clearer naming and future growth.
Existing templates remain compatible or have a controlled migration path.
Frontend, backend and tests use the same canonical variable registry.

milestones.title: Discovery and runtime contract
milestones.target_outcome: Current runtime context and rendering contract are documented and clarified.

milestones.title: Canonical registry and semantics
milestones.target_outcome: Canonical variables, scopes, naming and alias semantics are implemented.

milestones.title: UX, tests and rollout safety
milestones.target_outcome: API/UI exposure, preview, diagnostics, tests and migration guidance are complete.

tasks.title: Current variable model document
acceptance_criteria: A short internal document lists all currently allowed variables from the backend.
The document explains which ones are legacy, which are generic and which are domain-specific.
Current ambiguity and naming inconsistencies are explicitly identified.

tasks.title: Canonical variable registry introduce
acceptance_criteria: Allowed template variables move into one central registry instead of a flat route-local constant.
Each variable has metadata such as name, description, scope and stability level.
Frontend and backend can consume the same canonical list.

tasks.title: Variable scopes define
acceptance_criteria: Variables are grouped by context such as agent, task, team, role, blueprint, artifact or domain-specific extras.
The registry can express in which contexts a variable is valid.
Validation can distinguish globally known variables from contextually valid variables.

tasks.title: Naming scheme clean up
acceptance_criteria: A clear naming policy is defined for future variables.
Core names are normalized consistently instead of mixed German/English naming.
Legacy aliases can remain temporarily for compatibility.

tasks.title: Legacy alias strategy define
acceptance_criteria: Existing variables such as German aliases are mapped to canonical names where needed.
The system can warn on legacy aliases without breaking existing templates immediately.
A migration path is documented.

tasks.title: Context-aware validation improve
acceptance_criteria: Validation can report not only unknown variables but also variables that are known yet unavailable in the current context.
Validation errors and warnings are more precise than today.
Strict mode still works with the improved semantics.

tasks.title: Explain variable meaning and origin
acceptance_criteria: Each variable has a human-readable description.
The system can explain where the value comes from and when it may be empty.
This information is available to UI and docs.

tasks.title: Expose registry through API
acceptance_criteria: There is an API or config read-model path that returns the canonical variable registry.
The payload includes name, description, scope and deprecated/alias state where applicable.
The frontend no longer hardcodes the visible variable descriptions.

tasks.title: Frontend variable help improve
acceptance_criteria: The template editor shows allowed variables grouped by scope.
Each variable has tooltip/help text.
Unknown and context-invalid variables are clearly distinguished.

tasks.title: Template render preview add
acceptance_criteria: Users can preview how a template would render with sample values.
Missing variables are visible in preview mode.
The preview does not require saving the template first.

tasks.title: Sample context payloads define
acceptance_criteria: Representative example payloads exist for task, team, role, blueprint and agent contexts.
These payloads are used for preview, docs and tests.
The samples make variable availability concrete.

tasks.title: Blueprint and team integration audit
acceptance_criteria: The code paths where templates are actually used from blueprints, roles, teams and members are reviewed.
The audit identifies which context objects are truly available at render time.
The variable registry is aligned with real runtime availability.

tasks.title: Runtime rendering contract define
acceptance_criteria: A clear contract defines how templates are rendered at runtime and with which context object.
The contract distinguishes admin-time validation from runtime rendering.
The contract is documented and testable.

tasks.title: Tests for canonical variable registry add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify the canonical registry content and shape.
Tests cover aliases, deprecated names and per-scope availability.
Tests protect frontend/backend consistency.

tasks.title: Tests for context-aware validation add
acceptance_criteria: Validation tests distinguish unknown, deprecated and context-invalid variables.
Strict mode behavior remains covered.
Regression tests exist for current known variables.

tasks.title: Documentation for template authors add
acceptance_criteria: There is a dedicated guide for writing templates with variables.
The guide includes available variables, scopes, examples and warnings.
The guide is linked from the template UI or admin docs.

tasks.title: Migration notes for existing templates prepare
acceptance_criteria: There is a migration note for existing templates that rely on current variable names.
Potential breaking changes are listed clearly.
A transition plan exists for legacy names.

tasks.title: Operator diagnostics for template validation improve
acceptance_criteria: Operators can inspect why a template failed validation or rendered incompletely.
Diagnostics remain safe and do not leak sensitive runtime values by default.
Validation problems are easier to debug than today.
